programmes.show_description: David Wallace Lockhart, Natalie Higgins and Phil Sim on the week's political news.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
Alison Jack and Linden Bicket find meaning in the human suffering at the heart of the Good Friday story and explore its enduring impact in turbulent and anxious times.
The life story of Jesus can seem distant, but as we face the fears of our current world, can the meaning of Good Friday offer ways to peace and grace in an uncertain age?
News from across Scotland and the world, plus sport, weather, entertainment and big name interviews.
Stephen Jardine gets to the heart of the stories making the news.
News updates from Scotland and beyond, plus all your lunchtime sport, travel and weather.
Des Clarke hosts Scotland's award-winning satirical news quiz.
Nicola Meighan brings you feel-good music and chat with celebrity guests.
Breaking down the day's talking points with comment and opinion, plus sport, travel and weather.
New and classic cuts all played from 7', 12' and vinyl LPs. Grant shares the treasures from his own shopping adventures and discovers the stories about your record collections.
Phoebe and Steph bring you the best new music coming out of Scotland and reflect on Alice Faye winning BBC Introducing's Scottish Act of the Year.
The best company to end the day, with lively chat and some of your favourite tunes.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
Mark Stephen and Calum Maclean with stories from the great outdoors.
News from across Scotland and the world, plus sport, weather, entertainment and big name interviews.
Amy Irons and Steven Mill kick-start your Saturday with lots of laughs, great guests and timeless tunes as we look back on the week's news and preview the day's football fixtures.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
Keep up to date with all the goal action from across the country in Open All Mics. (Including option of Rangers v Dundee United commentary).
Full commentary live from Ibrox as Rangers take on Dundee Utd (KO 1500).
Live radio commentary of Glasgow Warriors v Bulls in the Investec Champions Cup.
For the very best in traditional music, join Gary Innes for Take the Floor. We have a brilliant session from Fin Hope and his band.
Mìcheal Steele with a selection of the best in pipe music, this week he features sets from Canadian pipers Bruce Gandy, Michael Grey, Jack Lee, amongst many others.
Acclaimed Scottish jazz singer-songwriter Georgia Cécile goes On the Record, and this week's Heavy Rotation album is from Courtney Barnett.
